title: Crispy Crunchy Peruvian Fish Stir-fry
description: A savory, paleo-friendly Peruvian-inspired stirfry featuring lightly battered fish fillets tossed with crunchy corn and fresh herbs, perfect for feeding a crowd.

ingredients.name: spices (cumin, smoked paprika, salt, pepper)
ingredients.notes: 1 tsp each of cumin, smoked paprika, salt, pepper

instructions: Pat the fish fillets dry with paper towels and set aside.
In a shallow bowl, whisk together almond flour, potato starch, and the spice blend until well combined.
In a second bowl, beat the egg until smooth.
Dip each fillet first in the flour mixture, then in the beaten egg, and back into the flour mixture to create a double coat.
Heat olive oil and coconut oil together in a large skillet over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add the coated fillets and fry for 3‑4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and cooked through. Transfer to a plate lined with paper towels.
In the same skillet, add the minced garlic and sliced onion; sauté for 2 minutes until the onion is translucent.
Add the sliced bell pepper and cook for another 2 minutes.
Stir in the roasted corn kernels and cook for 1 minute, then return the fish fillets to the pan and toss gently to combine.
Drizzle lime juice over the stir‑fry, sprinkle chopped cilantro, and serve immediately.
